transcript: te瑟k f蓴藧r 藞伞r忙nt瑟d
definitions: To fail to appreciate something or someone because it has always been there.
To assume something is true without question or proof.
examples: Don't take your health for granted.
She took his help for granted.
We often take clean water for granted.
He took it for granted that she would agree.
pastTenses: took for granted, taken for granted
